result:
<<<KOT:equalitarianism>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"equalitarianism is a school of thought within political philosophy — an abstract doctrine built on claims about moral equality, rights, and justice. Its identity depends on normative theories about what ought to be true of societies, not on observable facts expressible in the prime lexicon. The concept requires reference to complex institutional structures, legal frameworks, and philosophical arguments that lie outside the semantic scope of the Kernel of Truth catalogue."}
<<<END:equalitarianism>>>>

<<<KOT:examinee>>>>
{"kind":"molecule","gloss":"a someone who is undergoing an examination; someone else does this examination; it is done to know something about the examinee.","groundingNote":"a someone who is undergoing an examination; someone else does this examination; it is done to know something about the examinee.","groundingRefs":[]}
<<<END:examinee>>>>

<<<KOT:falconine>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"falconine is a relational adjective denoting resemblance to or kinship with falcons — a zoological kind. Expressing 'of or relating to falcons' requires either a direct reference to the falcon kind (which would need to be in the molecule catalog, but the catalog provides only urn:molecule-v0:bird, not a specific falcon entry) or a comparative structure ('like a falcon') that presupposes the target kind is already grounded. The concept is fundamentally taxonomic and cannot be reduced to prime predicates and observable properties without assuming prior formal definition of the falcon kind."}
<<<END:falconine>>>>

<<<KOT:filaggrin>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"filaggrin is a technical biochemistry concept — a specific filament-associated protein with molecular identity determined by its amino-acid sequence, post-translational processing, genetic encoding (FLG gene), and cellular function. Its definition requires reference to molecular biology (keratin, epithelial differentiation, proteolysis) and genetics that lie entirely outside the prime lexicon and the Kernel catalogue. It belongs to the biochemistry and molecular-genetics sector, not the everyday semantic tier."}
<<<END:filaggrin>>>>
